mnemonics: CISCO - Clock, Inputs, State, Combinational, Outputs (for sequential circuit components)
My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nachos - Machine, Virtual, External, Main, JVM, Swap, Unified, NUMA (for memory hierarchy)
Every Good Burger Deserves Fries - Execute, Decode, Fetch, Back (for instruction cycle phases in reverse order)
keyPoints: The difference between combinational (output depends only on current inputs) and sequential logic (output depends on current inputs and state)
The von Neumann architecture model with its stored-program concept and single memory for instructions and data
The memory hierarchy exists to create the illusion of fast, large, cheap memory through caching and tiered storage
commonMistakes: Confusing memory addressability (byte vs word) and calculating address space incorrectly
Assuming all instructions take the same number of clock cycles to execute in a simple processor
Mixing up the roles of the program counter (PC) and instruction register (IR) during fetch-execute cycle
